sampled.snippet: When considering images divided by reference location or compression type, etc., a few IQMs perform statistically better than RMSE and SSIM, and even differences in performance among the better-performing IQMs become statistically significant. The IQMs which perform best for prediction of subjective scores in our study are based on the structural similarity (SSIM, FSIM, IWSSIM) and visual information fidelity (VIF, NQM, GMSD, HDRVDP) approaches.

observed.at_claimed_location: IV. CONCLUSION actually reads: 'We measured the correlations between 10 full-reference objective IQMs and the subjective image quality score of five subspecialty radiologists. When considering images divided by reference location or combining all images in the study, SSIM and RMSE demonstrated statistically worse performances than other metrics evaluated, suggesting that SSIM and RMSE are potentially not ideal surrogate measures of MR image quality as determined by radiologist evaluation. The VIF, FSIM, and NQM demonstrated the highest correlation with radiologists' opinions of MR image quality. However, these metrics come at the cost of longer calculation times...'
observed.note: Sampled snippet does NOT appear in IV. CONCLUSION (or in any other section per grep of distinctive phrases 'divided by reference location or compression type', 'structural similarity (SSIM, FSIM, IWSSIM)'). The sampled snippet appears to be a paraphrase/fabrication: (a) substitutes 'compression type' for the paper's 'combining all images in the study'; (b) invents an IQM-family taxonomy ('structural similarity' family of SSIM/FSIM/IWSSIM and 'visual information fidelity' family of VIF/NQM/GMSD/HDRVDP) that the source's IV. CONCLUSION does not present (and which is technically inaccurate, e.g. NQM/GMSD/HDRVDP are not VIF-family metrics). The substantive sub-claim itself remains supported by the *actual* IV. CONCLUSION text and other evidence in the entry; the failure is in this single sampled evidence snippet's verbatim accuracy, not in the verdict.
